headline: Military victory, political stalemate: The US's strategic myopia in the Middle East
description: Two weeks after launching the war against Iran alongside Israel, the Trump administration appears not to have anticipated Iranian strikes against neighboring countries, the closure of the Strait of Hormuz or the organized succession within the Iranian regime. It is also becoming clear that the two allies do not share the same priorities.

headline: After soldier is killed in Iraq, France confronts costs of Middle East engagement
description: Emmanuel Macron announced the death of a French service member in a drone strike claimed by a pro-Iranian armed group. Six other soldiers were wounded.

headline: In Lebanon, the fear of Israeli strikes against Hezbollah spreads to downtown Beirut
description: The heart of the Lebanese capital has come under Israeli strikes. Early Thursday morning, along the corniche promenade, a double drone strike on a car killed 12 people. Residential buildings believed to be sheltering Hezbollah members were also targeted.

headline: Pritzker Prize honors Chilean Smiljan Radic, architect of interiority
description: Ten years after Alejandro Aravena, Radic has become the second Chilean to be awarded what is considered the Nobel Prize of architecture.

headline: Races to watch in France's 2026 municipal elections
description: Aside from major cities like Paris and Marseille, the local election results from smaller towns like Le Havre and Menton are expected to reveal early political trends ahead of the 2027 presidential and legislative contests.

Jeffrey Epstein's Parisian lives
description: Over 18 years, the extremely kitsch property at 22 Avenue Foch served Epstein as a European office, a salon for artists and scientists and, above all, one of the places where he committed his sexual crimes.

headline: 'The Islamism of the Iranian regime is now reduced to a rather worn veneer'
description: The appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei as supreme leader, in a regime that calls itself an Islamic Republic, already signals a form of defeat in the ongoing war waged by the US and Israel, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

headline: An exhibition where art confronts death
description: The Pinault Collection's new exhibition at the Bourse de Commerce, 'Clair-obscur,' features some 100 works by 27 artists that explore the theme of death.
